allies.rationale_text: Scaffolding supports six-thinking-hats by making complex review work manageable through temporary structure and role separation.
allies.source_quote: This model acts as cognitive scaffolding.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Six-thinking-hats conflicts with anchoring when fixation on initial information limits real perspective rotation.
structured_tensions.source_quote: six-thinking-hats vs anchoring: six-thinking-hats conflicts with anchoring (anchoring) when anchoring can limit the flexibility of thought that Six Thinking Hats encourages by fixating on initial information.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Six-thinking-hats conflicts with commitment-bias when people stay attached to one perspective instead of exploring multiple viewpoints.
structured_tensions.source_quote: six-thinking-hats vs commitment-bias: six-thinking-hats conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when commitment Bias may conflict with the Six Thinking Hats' approach by causing individuals to stick to one perspective rather than exploring multiple viewpoints.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Six-thinking-hats conflicts with confirmation-bias when teams default to their initial hypothesis instead of genuinely switching hats.
structured_tensions.source_quote: six-thinking-hats vs confirmation-bias: six-thinking-hats conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when teams default to their initial hypothesis rather than genuinely adopting each hat's perspective to pressure-test alternatives.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the relation surface tight around scaffolding and the explicit anti-dominant-frame tension block.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader bias or role-based relation sprawl beyond the clearest current-vocabulary links.
open_questions: The outside-view material is source-real but not yet a clean direct canonical target here.
